agentMessage.excerpt:
Review checklist → pass
✓  Build completed — the implementor finished in 20 round(s)
✓  Change is visible — 3 file(s) changed: docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md, README.md, docs/API.md
✓  Changed the deliverable's target files — expected ≥1 of: README.md, docs/API.md, docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md
✓  Verification passed — the project verify command was GREEN
✓  Work committed — committed locally on the build branch
0.90  Implements the approved plan — Covers all three acceptance criteria: stable contract documented, deprecation policy with staged process (announcement, grace period, removal), and changelog expectation. Minor divergence: plan specified renames with both old/new IDs served concurrently, while doc takes immutable-ID approach (IDs never change on rebrand). This still achieves the stability guarantee and is a defensible design choice, but doesn't precisely match the plan's stated mechanism.
1.00  Minimal and scoped — Only three files changed: the new docs/PROVIDER_IDS.md, a single link line in README.md, and a small 'See also' section in docs/API.md. All are documentation-only edits exactly as the plan called for. No source code touched, no unrelated churn.
1.00  No test-gaming / faking — Content is substantive: 62+ lines with a provider ID table (10+ entries), explicit stability guarantees, a 3-stage deprecation/removal policy with timeline, changelog expectations, and a FAQ section. No placeholder text, no tests weakened or deleted to force green.
1.00  Safe to advance — Documentation is accurate and poses no correctness or security risk. It sensibly describes immutable provider IDs, a 90-day deprecation window, and changelog notification expectations. No misleading or dangerous claims.
0.95  Judge confidence — at or above the confidence bar
